item.title: Ebola raises questions over pandemic preparedness
item.description: Aid cuts and US hostility to concept of global public health endanger us all

item.title: Wendy’s is a prime candidate for the Nelson Peltz activist special
item.description: One-third drop in the fast-food chain’s share price has created another opportunity for Trian

item.title: America’s ailing one-trick pony
item.description: Trump’s excessive faith in military power is squarely within the US tradition

item.title: Vibe shift, continued
item.description: Plus, more on gilts

item.title: Why politicians struggle to read the public on immigration
item.description: The government overreacts to popular opinion, which prompts a pendulum swing the other way

item.title: Labour’s lack of intellectual rigour has returned to bite it
item.description: Starmer and his party failed to do any hard thinking in opposition and they are now paying the price

item.title: Worries on gilts point to wider bond market vulnerabilities
item.description: Rising public debt adds pressure while increased hedge fund trading opens up new fragilities

item.title: Inside the luxury towers behind Manchester’s revival
item.description: Salboy, the developer co-founded by Betfred billionaire Fred Done, has helped to transform the city

item.title: The US and China crave hegemony without responsibility
item.description: Beijing has yet to step forward to fill the leadership vacuum left by Washington

item.title: SpaceX IPO adds air to the Silicon Valley ‘genius bubble’
item.description: Today’s biggest one-man brands benefit from the impression that no cycle can bring them down

item.title: The cracks in the Putin edifice
item.description: Russian leader’s conviction that he could outlast the west is being tested

item.title: How Trump became political kryptonite
item.description: The president’s ‘populist war’ in Iran is dooming his allies and leaders who sought his favour

item.title: NextEra bets there’s no such thing as having too much power
item.description: There are few obvious areas of overlap between Dominion Energy and its Florida rival

item.title: Baseball is learning to live with shorter attention spans
item.description: New rules have made games faster and more exciting

item.title: Tank maker KNDS comes late to European defence boom
item.description: Franco-German group might fare better reviving a previously rejected alliance with Rheinmetall

item.title: The UK must embrace its libraries in the age of AI
item.description: As stewards of vast quantities of data, the sector could play a critical role in fuelling the digital economy

item.title: Trump and Xi will not determine Taiwan’s fate
item.description: China assumes that the island would be helpless without American support — that is a dangerous mistake

item.title: Bonds bad
item.description: Plus, stocks bad

item.title: The battle the US is winning against China
item.description: It is a stunted financial power, leaving the US free to rule global markets

item.title: The Italianisation of Britain’s finances
item.description: Investors have watched UK political flip-flopping and budgetary shortfalls with alarm

item.title: The truth behind the manosphere’s ‘looksmaxxed’ interiors
item.description: The white, polished, supersized properties of Andrew Tate et al reveal something radical that even ‘The Matrix’ did not manage

item.title: Leaders’ judgment matters more than ever in the age of AI
item.description: A professor explores how to hone abilities too rarely nurtured in business schools

item.title: How to stay ambitious when there is no ladder left to climb
item.description: In retirement, traditional markers of success become murkier and structure must come from yourself

item.title: Asia is headed towards an industrial supercycle
item.description: It’s not just AI — energy, defence and industrial capex will fuel growth

item.title: The looming energy crunch
item.description: Supply squeeze due to the Iran war will get worse before it gets better
